zh.Orléans, Orléanais, France: 法國奧爾萊納薩奧爾萊安斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor Grey於大約1381年出生在威爾士丹比爾盧時，她的父親Reynold Grey II20歲，母親Margaret de Ros22歲。她於1397年10月22日之前與Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1434年之前，埋葬在英國倫敦特福利亞斯。

zh-hans.Orléans, Orléanais, France: 法国奧爾萊納薩奧爾萊安斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor Grey于大约1381年出生在威尔士丹比爾盧时，她的父亲Reynold Grey II20岁，母亲Margaret de Ros22岁。她于1397年10月22日之前与Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1434年之前，埋葬在英国倫敦特福利亞斯。

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor Grey was born about 1381, in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales, her father, Reynold Grey II, was 20 and her mother, Margaret de Ros, was 22. She married Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings before 22 October 1397. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died before 1434, and was buried in Whitefriars, London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor Grey est née environ 1381, à Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles, son père, Reynold Grey II, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Ros, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings avant 22 octobre 1397. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée avant 1434, et a été enterrée à Whitefriars, Londres, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor Grey um 1381 in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Reynold Grey II 20 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Ros 22. Sie hat vor 22. Oktober 1397 Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb vor 1434 und wurde in Whitefriars, London, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor Grey è nata il circa 1381 nel Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles, sua padre, Reynold Grey II, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Ros, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings prima del 22 ottobre 1397. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì prima del 1434, e fu sepolta a Whitefriars, London, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor Greyが約1381年にウェールズデンビグhシュアイアルスインで生まれた時，父Reynold Grey IIは20歳，母Margaret de Rosは22歳であった。1397年10月22日以前にRobert Poynings 5th Baron Poyningsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1434年以前に死去し，イギリスロンドンウィテフリアルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Grey(이)가 대략 1381년 웨일스 덴비그h실르 루스인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Reynold Grey II(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Ros(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1397년 10월 22일 이전 Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1434년 이전 사망했으며 영국 런던 휘테프리아르스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor Grey nasceu aproximadamente 1381, em Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales, seu pai, Reynold Grey II, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Ros, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings antes 22 de outubro de 1397. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu antes 1434, e foi sepultada em Whitefriars, London, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor Grey родилась около 1381 г. в местности рутин, денбаишир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Reynold Grey II было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Ros, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings перед 22 октябрь 1397 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла перед 1434 г. и была похоронена в местности уитефрярс, Лондон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor Grey nació aproximadamente 1381, en Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales, su papá, Reynold Grey II, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Margaret de Ros, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Robert Poynings 5th Baron Poynings antes de 22 de octubre de 1397. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió antes de 1434, y fue sepultada en Whitefriars, London, Inglaterra.
